---
title: "Selection"
format: 
  revealjs:
    incremental: true
    callout-icon: false
    code-overflow: scroll
    code-block-height: 200px
    #theme: [default, ../custom.scss]
#julia:
#  path: "/home/emmanuel/.juliaup/bin/julia"
smaller: false
css: ../../../styles.css
#filters:
  #- parse-latex
---




# Model Selection

::: {style="display:none"}
$\newcommand{\VS}{\quad \mathrm{VS} \quad}$
$\newcommand{\and}{\quad \mathrm{and} \quad}$
$\newcommand{\E}{\mathbb E}$
$\newcommand{\P}{\mathbb P}$
$\newcommand{\Var}{\mathbb V}$
$\newcommand{\1}{\mathbf 1}$
:::


## Practical Question

. . .

In practice, we often hesitate between several models:

- Which variables to include in the model?
- How to choose between one model and another?
- **Ideally**: How to select the "best" model among all possible sub-models of a large linear regression model?

## Selection Criteria

. . .

Several criteria exist. The main ones:

- **$R_a^2$**: Adjusted $R^2$ (already seen)
- **Fisher test** for nested models (already seen)  
- **Mallows' $C_p$**
- **AIC criterion**
- **BIC criterion**


## Setup

. . .

Suppose [we have $p_{\max}$ explanatory variables]{style="background-color: yellow;"}, forming the "maximal" design matrix $X_{\max}$.

. . .

**True model** (unknown):

::: {.square-def}
$$Y = X^*\beta^* + \varepsilon$$
:::


where [$X^*$ is a sub-matrix of $X_{\max}$ formed by $p^* \leq p_{\max}$ columns]{style="background-color: yellow;"}.

. . .

[We don't know $p^*$]{style="background-color: yellow;"} nor which variables are involved.

**Goal**: Select the correct matrix $X^*$ and **estimate** $\beta^*$.

## Practice

. . .

We [regress $Y$ on $p \leq p_{\max}$ variables]{style="background-color: yellow;"}, assuming:
$$Y = X\beta + \varepsilon$$
where $X$: sub-matrix of $X_{\max}$ containing the [$p$ chosen columns]{style="background-color: yellow;"} (yielding $\hat{\beta}$).

. . .

This model is **potentially wrong** (bad choice of variables).

. . .

**Objective**: Calculate a [quality score]{style="background-color: yellow;"} for this submodel.


# The selection criteria

## Adjusted R² - Reminder

. . .

For a model with constant:

::: {.square-def}
$$R_a^2 = 1 - \frac{n-1}{n-p} \cdot \frac{SSR}{SST}$$
:::


- $SST = \sum_{i=1}^n (Y_i - \bar{Y})^2$ (independent of chosen model)
- $SSR = \sum_{i=1}^n (Y_i - \hat{Y}_i)^2$ (specific to considered model)

. . .

**Selection rule**: Between two models, prefer **highest** $R_a^2$.

## A submodel = a subset of columns {.smaller}

. . .


::: {style="font-size:86%"}
A submodel keeps a **subset of the columns** of the maximal design $X_{\max}$ (blue = kept, magenta = dropped). Here the columns are powers $1,x,x^2,\dots$; slide $p$ to keep more (nested): $R^2$ can only rise, while $R^2_a$ improves **only if the added column is useful** — over-selecting at random doesn't pay off (motivating the criteria next).
:::

```{=html}
<style>
.lm-btn{font-family:inherit;font-size:.85em;padding:.4em 1.1em;margin:.12em .22em;border:1px solid #bbb;border-radius:8px;background:#f4f4f4;color:#333;cursor:pointer}
.lm-row{font-size:.72em;line-height:2.3;margin:.12rem 0}
.lm-wrap{text-align:center}
.lm-sw{display:inline-block;width:11px;height:11px;border-radius:2px;vertical-align:middle}
</style>
<div class="lm-wrap">
  <canvas id="seCanvas" width="660" height="300" style="background:#fff;border:1px solid #ddd;border-radius:8px;max-width:100%"></canvas>
  <div class="lm-row">kept columns p <input id="seP" type="range" autocomplete="off" min="1" max="8" step="1" value="4" style="vertical-align:middle;width:170px"> <b id="sePv">4</b> &nbsp;·&nbsp; noise <input id="seNz" type="range" autocomplete="off" min="0" max="0.5" step="0.01" value="0.15" style="vertical-align:middle;width:90px"> <b id="seNzv">0.15</b> &nbsp; <button class="lm-btn" id="seNew">↻ reshuffle</button></div>
  <div class="lm-row"><span class="lm-sw" style="background:#3b6fb6"></span> kept &nbsp; <span class="lm-sw" style="background:#c0508f"></span> dropped &nbsp;·&nbsp; R²=<b id="seR2">–</b> &nbsp;·&nbsp; R²ₐ=<b id="seR2a">–</b> &nbsp;·&nbsp; <span style="color:#888">true size p*=3</span></div>
</div>
<script>
(function () {
  var cv = document.getElementById('seCanvas'); if (!cv || cv.dataset.init) return; cv.dataset.init = 1;
  var c = cv.getContext('2d'), W = cv.width, H = cv.height, PMAX = 8, N = 30, Xp = [], Zp = [], order = [];
  var LBL = ['1', 'x', 'x²', 'x³', 'x⁴', 'x⁵', 'x⁶', 'x⁷'], TRUE = [0.3, 1.0, 0.7];   // true model: powers 0..2 (degree 2)
  var pEl = document.getElementById('seP'), nzEl = document.getElementById('seNz');
  function gauss() { var u = 0, v = 0; while (!u) u = Math.random(); while (!v) v = Math.random(); return Math.sqrt(-2 * Math.log(u)) * Math.cos(2 * Math.PI * v); }
  function vdc(n) { var r = 0, d = 1; while (n > 0) { d *= 2; r += (n % 2) / d; n = Math.floor(n / 2); } return r; }
  function regen() { Xp = []; Zp = []; for (var i = 0; i < N; i++) { Xp.push(-1 + 2 * vdc(i)); Zp.push(gauss()); } order = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7]; for (var j = order.length - 1; j > 0; j--) { var k = (Math.random() * (j + 1)) | 0, t = order[j]; order[j] = order[k]; order[k] = t; } }
  function trueY(x) { var s = 0; for (var k = 0; k < TRUE.length; k++) s += TRUE[k] * Math.pow(x, k); return s; }
  function solve(M, b) { var n = b.length, a = M.map(function (r) { return r.slice(); }), bb = b.slice(), i, r, k; for (i = 0; i < n; i++) { var pv = i; for (r = i + 1; r < n; r++) if (Math.abs(a[r][i]) > Math.abs(a[pv][i])) pv = r; var t = a[i]; a[i] = a[pv]; a[pv] = t; var tb = bb[i]; bb[i] = bb[pv]; bb[pv] = tb; for (r = i + 1; r < n; r++) { var f = a[r][i] / (a[i][i] || 1e-12); for (k = i; k < n; k++) a[r][k] -= f * a[i][k]; bb[r] -= f * bb[i]; } } var x = new Array(n).fill(0); for (i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) { var s = bb[i]; for (k = i + 1; k < n; k++) s -= a[i][k] * x[k]; x[i] = s / (a[i][i] || 1e-12); } return x; }
  function selectedPowers(p) { var sel = [0]; for (var j = 0; j < p - 1; j++) sel.push(order[j]); return sel.sort(function (a, b) { return a - b; }); }   // intercept + (p-1) random columns
  function fit(pows, Y) { var m = pows.length, M = [], v = [], i, r, k; for (r = 0; r < m; r++) { M.push(new Array(m).fill(0)); v.push(0); } for (i = 0; i < N; i++) { var row = pows.map(function (pw) { return Math.pow(Xp[i], pw); }); for (r = 0; r < m; r++) { for (k = 0; k < m; k++) M[r][k] += row[r] * row[k]; v[r] += row[r] * Y[i]; } } for (r = 0; r < m; r++) M[r][r] += 1e-7; return solve(M, v); }
  function evalFit(pows, beta, x) { var s = 0; for (var j = 0; j < pows.length; j++) s += beta[j] * Math.pow(x, pows[j]); return s; }
  function draw() {
    var p = +pEl.value, nz = +nzEl.value; document.getElementById('sePv').textContent = p; document.getElementById('seNzv').textContent = nz.toFixed(2);
    var sel = selectedPowers(p), selSet = {}; sel.forEach(function (k) { selSet[k] = 1; });
    var Y = Xp.map(function (x, i) { return trueY(x) + nz * Zp[i]; });
    var beta = fit(sel, Y);
    c.clearRect(0, 0, W, H);
    // LEFT: design matrix schematic
    var mx0 = 16, mtop = 16, mh = 210, cw = (236 - mx0) / PMAX;
    c.font = '11px sans-serif'; c.textAlign = 'center'; c.fillStyle = '#777'; c.fillText('columns of X (powers)', mx0 + PMAX * cw / 2 - 4, mtop - 4);
    for (var k = 0; k < PMAX; k++) {
      c.fillStyle = selSet[k] ? '#3b6fb6' : '#c0508f'; c.globalAlpha = selSet[k] ? 0.9 : 0.5;
      c.fillRect(mx0 + k * cw + 1, mtop, cw - 3, mh); c.globalAlpha = 1;
      c.fillStyle = '#333'; c.fillText(LBL[k], mx0 + k * cw + cw / 2, mtop + mh + 14);
    }
    // RIGHT: data + true (dashed) + fit
    var rx0 = 300, rx1 = W - 12, XL = -1.12, XH = 1.12, YL = -1.9, YH = 1.9, i;
    function PX(x) { return rx0 + (x - XL) / (XH - XL) * (rx1 - rx0); } function PY(y) { return H - 24 - (y - YL) / (YH - YL) * (H - 38); }
    c.strokeStyle = '#ddd'; c.strokeRect(rx0, 12, rx1 - rx0, H - 36); c.fillStyle = '#777'; c.textAlign = 'center'; c.fillText('fit with the kept columns', (rx0 + rx1) / 2, H - 8);
    c.strokeStyle = '#bbb'; c.lineWidth = 1.5; c.setLineDash([5, 4]); c.beginPath(); for (i = 0; i <= 200; i++) { var x = XL + (XH - XL) * i / 200; i ? c.lineTo(PX(x), PY(trueY(x))) : c.moveTo(PX(x), PY(trueY(x))); } c.stroke(); c.setLineDash([]);
    c.save(); c.beginPath(); c.rect(rx0, 12, rx1 - rx0, H - 36); c.clip();
    c.strokeStyle = '#e74c3c'; c.lineWidth = 2.5; c.beginPath(); for (i = 0; i <= 220; i++) { var xx = XL + (XH - XL) * i / 220; i ? c.lineTo(PX(xx), PY(evalFit(sel, beta, xx))) : c.moveTo(PX(xx), PY(evalFit(sel, beta, xx))); } c.stroke();
    c.fillStyle = 'rgba(44,62,80,0.7)'; for (i = 0; i < N; i++) { c.beginPath(); c.arc(PX(Xp[i]), PY(Y[i]), 2.8, 0, 6.2832); c.fill(); }
    c.restore();
    var my = 0; Y.forEach(function (y) { my += y; }); my /= N; var ssr = 0, sst = 0;
    Y.forEach(function (y, i) { var yh = evalFit(sel, beta, Xp[i]); ssr += (y - yh) * (y - yh); sst += (y - my) * (y - my); });
    document.getElementById('seR2').textContent = (1 - ssr / sst).toFixed(3); document.getElementById('seR2a').textContent = (1 - (N - 1) / (N - p) * ssr / sst).toFixed(3);
  }
  pEl.addEventListener('input', draw); nzEl.addEventListener('input', draw);
  document.getElementById('seNew').addEventListener('click', function () { regen(); draw(); });
  regen(); draw();
})();
</script>
```

## Fisher Test for Nested Models

. . .


::: {.square-def}
$$F = \frac{n-p}{q} \cdot \frac{SSR_c - SSR}{SSR}$$
:::



- $SSR$: residual sum of squares of the **larger model**
- $SSR_c$: SSR of the **sub-model** (fewer variables)
- $p$: number of variables in the larger model
- $q$: number of constraints ($p-q$ variables in sub-model)

. . .

If $F < f_{q,n-p}(1-\alpha)$: prefer **sub-model** ($H_0$ at level $\alpha$)


## Mallows' Cₚ

- **True model** (unknown): $Y = X^*\beta^* + \varepsilon$  
- **Tested model** (possibly wrong): $Y = X\beta + \varepsilon$ with OLS estimate $\hat{\beta}$

. . .

Mallows' $C_p$ aims to estimate the **prediction risk**:
$$\E(\|\tilde{Y} - X\hat{\beta}\|^2)$$


where $\tilde{Y}$ follows the same distribution as $Y$ but is independent.

## Formula for Mallows' Cₚ
. . .

::: {.square-def}
$C_p = \frac{SSR}{\hat{\sigma}^2} - n + 2p$
:::



- $p$: number of variables in the considered model
- $SSR$: residual sum of squares of the considered model  
- $\hat{\sigma}^2$: estimation of $\sigma^2$ in the **largest model**
 - Same for all tested models

. . .

**Selection rule**: Among all tested models, choose the one with **lowest** $C_p$.


## AIC Criterion

. . .

AIC (Akaike Information Criterion) is motivated like $C_p$.

It also focuses on prediction error $\tilde{Y} - X\hat{\beta}$, but **Kullback distance** instead of **Quadratic distance**.

. . .

::: {.square-def}
$$AIC = n \ln\left(\frac{SSR}{n}\right) + 2(p+1)$$
:::

. . .

**Selection rule**: choose model with **lowest** AIC.

In practice, **AIC and $C_p$ are very close** (choose same model)


## BIC Criterion

. . .

BIC (Bayesian Information Criterion) seeks the **"most probable" model** in a Bayesian formalism.

::: {.square-def}
$$BIC = n \ln\left(\frac{SSR}{n}\right) + (p+1) \ln n$$
:::

. . .

**Selection rule**: choose the one with **lowest** BIC.

- **Key difference**: The "2" in front of $(p+1)$ is replaced by $\ln n$
- This difference **frequently** leads to a [different model choice between AIC and BIC]{style="background-color: yellow;"}

# Comparing the criteria

## The fit–complexity trade-off

- **"Large" model**: low $SSR$, but high number of variables $p$ \
 (if too large: overfitting)
- **"Small" model**: high $SSR$, but low number of variables $p$ \ 
  (if too small: underfitting)

. . .

All previous criteria try to find a **compromise** between:

- **Good fit** to data (low $SSR$)
- **Small model size** (low $p$)

. . .

This is a **permanent trade-off** in statistics (not just in regression).

## The trade-off, term by term {.smaller}

. . .


::: {style="font-size:84%"}
A criterion is $f(\mathrm{SSR}) + c(n)\,p$: the **fit term** $n\ln(\mathrm{SSR}/n)$ falls as the model grows, the **penalty** $c(n)\,p$ rises. Their **sum** is U-shaped — its minimum is the selected size. Bigger $c(n)$ (BIC vs AIC) ⇒ smaller model.
:::

```{=html}
<style>
.lm-btn{font-family:inherit;font-size:.82em;padding:.35em 1em;margin:.1em .18em;border:1px solid #bbb;border-radius:8px;background:#f4f4f4;color:#333;cursor:pointer}
.lm-btn.on{background:#3b6fb6;color:#fff;border-color:#3b6fb6}
.lm-row{font-size:.72em;line-height:2.3;margin:.12rem 0}
.lm-wrap{text-align:center}
.lm-sw{display:inline-block;width:11px;height:11px;border-radius:2px;vertical-align:middle}
</style>
<div class="lm-wrap" id="toWrap">
  <canvas id="toCanvas" width="620" height="300" style="background:#fff;border:1px solid #ddd;border-radius:8px;max-width:100%"></canvas>
  <div class="lm-row">criterion: <button class="lm-btn on" data-c="bic">BIC (c=ln n)</button><button class="lm-btn" data-c="aic">AIC (c=2)</button> &nbsp; noise <input id="toNz" type="range" autocomplete="off" min="0.05" max="0.6" step="0.01" value="0.2" style="vertical-align:middle;width:100px"> <b id="toNzv">0.20</b> &nbsp; <button class="lm-btn" id="toNew">↻ resample</button></div>
  <div class="lm-row"><span class="lm-sw" style="background:#3b6fb6"></span> fit term &nbsp; <span class="lm-sw" style="background:#e67e22"></span> penalty &nbsp; <span class="lm-sw" style="background:#222"></span> total &nbsp;·&nbsp; selected p = <b id="toP">–</b> &nbsp;<span style="color:#888">(true p*=3)</span></div>
</div>
<script>
(function () {
  var cv = document.getElementById('toCanvas'); if (!cv || cv.dataset.init) return; cv.dataset.init = 1;
  var c = cv.getContext('2d'), W = cv.width, H = cv.height, PMAX = 8, N = 30, Xp = [], Zp = [], crit = 'bic';
  var TRUE = [0.3, 1.0, 0.7], nzEl = document.getElementById('toNz');
  function gauss() { var u = 0, v = 0; while (!u) u = Math.random(); while (!v) v = Math.random(); return Math.sqrt(-2 * Math.log(u)) * Math.cos(2 * Math.PI * v); }
  function vdc(n) { var r = 0, d = 1; while (n > 0) { d *= 2; r += (n % 2) / d; n = Math.floor(n / 2); } return r; }
  function regen() { Xp = []; Zp = []; for (var i = 0; i < N; i++) { Xp.push(-1 + 2 * vdc(i)); Zp.push(gauss()); } }
  function trueY(x) { var s = 0; for (var k = 0; k < TRUE.length; k++) s += TRUE[k] * Math.pow(x, k); return s; }
  function solve(M, b) { var n = b.length, a = M.map(function (r) { return r.slice(); }), bb = b.slice(), i, r, k; for (i = 0; i < n; i++) { var pv = i; for (r = i + 1; r < n; r++) if (Math.abs(a[r][i]) > Math.abs(a[pv][i])) pv = r; var t = a[i]; a[i] = a[pv]; a[pv] = t; var tb = bb[i]; bb[i] = bb[pv]; bb[pv] = tb; for (r = i + 1; r < n; r++) { var f = a[r][i] / (a[i][i] || 1e-12); for (k = i; k < n; k++) a[r][k] -= f * a[i][k]; bb[r] -= f * bb[i]; } } var x = new Array(n).fill(0); for (i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) { var s = bb[i]; for (k = i + 1; k < n; k++) s -= a[i][k] * x[k]; x[i] = s / (a[i][i] || 1e-12); } return x; }
  function ssrDeg(d, Y) { var pows = []; for (var k = 0; k <= d; k++) pows.push(k); var m = pows.length, M = [], v = [], i, r, kk; for (r = 0; r < m; r++) { M.push(new Array(m).fill(0)); v.push(0); } for (i = 0; i < N; i++) { var row = pows.map(function (pw) { return Math.pow(Xp[i], pw); }); for (r = 0; r < m; r++) { for (kk = 0; kk < m; kk++) M[r][kk] += row[r] * row[kk]; v[r] += row[r] * Y[i]; } } for (r = 0; r < m; r++) M[r][r] += 1e-8; var b = solve(M, v), s = 0; for (i = 0; i < N; i++) { var yh = 0; for (kk = 0; kk < m; kk++) yh += b[kk] * Math.pow(Xp[i], pows[kk]); s += (Y[i] - yh) * (Y[i] - yh); } return s; }
  function draw() {
    document.getElementById('toNzv').textContent = (+nzEl.value).toFixed(2);
    var Y = Xp.map(function (x, i) { return trueY(x) + (+nzEl.value) * Zp[i]; });
    var cn = crit === 'bic' ? Math.log(N) : 2, fitT = [], pen = [], tot = [], p;
    for (p = 1; p <= PMAX; p++) { var ssr = Math.max(ssrDeg(p - 1, Y), 1e-9); var ft = N * Math.log(ssr / N), pe = cn * (p + 1); fitT.push(ft); pen.push(pe); tot.push(ft + pe); }
    var lo = Math.min.apply(0, fitT.concat(pen).concat(tot)), hi = Math.max.apply(0, fitT.concat(pen).concat(tot)), pad = (hi - lo) * 0.08; lo -= pad; hi += pad;
    var bp = 0; for (p = 1; p < PMAX; p++) if (tot[p] < tot[bp]) bp = p;
    var X0 = 40, Y0 = 12, BW = W - 52, BH = H - 46;
    function PX(pp) { return X0 + (pp - 1) / (PMAX - 1) * BW; } function PY(y) { return Y0 + (hi - y) / (hi - lo) * BH; }
    c.clearRect(0, 0, W, H); c.strokeStyle = '#eee'; c.strokeRect(X0, Y0, BW, BH);
    c.fillStyle = '#999'; c.font = '10px sans-serif'; c.textAlign = 'center'; for (p = 1; p <= PMAX; p++) c.fillText(p, PX(p), H - 18);
    c.fillText('model size p', X0 + BW / 2, H - 4);
    function curve(arr, col, w) { c.strokeStyle = col; c.lineWidth = w; c.beginPath(); for (var p = 1; p <= PMAX; p++) { var x = PX(p), y = PY(arr[p - 1]); p > 1 ? c.lineTo(x, y) : c.moveTo(x, y); } c.stroke(); for (p = 1; p <= PMAX; p++) { c.fillStyle = col; c.beginPath(); c.arc(PX(p), PY(arr[p - 1]), 2.5, 0, 6.2832); c.fill(); } }
    curve(fitT, '#3b6fb6', 2); curve(pen, '#e67e22', 2); curve(tot, '#222', 2.6);
    c.strokeStyle = '#aaa'; c.setLineDash([3, 3]); c.beginPath(); c.moveTo(PX(3), Y0); c.lineTo(PX(3), Y0 + BH); c.stroke(); c.setLineDash([]);
    c.fillStyle = '#222'; c.beginPath(); c.arc(PX(bp + 1), PY(tot[bp]), 5, 0, 6.2832); c.fill();
    document.getElementById('toP').textContent = bp + 1;
  }
  Array.prototype.forEach.call(document.querySelectorAll('#toWrap .lm-btn[data-c]'), function (b) { b.addEventListener('click', function () { crit = b.dataset.c; Array.prototype.forEach.call(document.querySelectorAll('#toWrap .lm-btn[data-c]'), function (x) { x.classList.toggle('on', x === b); }); draw(); }); });
  nzEl.addEventListener('input', draw);
  document.getElementById('toNew').addEventListener('click', function () { regen(); draw(); });
  regen(); draw();
})();
</script>
```

## General Form

. . .

$C_p$, AIC, and BIC consist of minimizing an expression of the form:

::: {.square-def}
$$f(SSR) + c(n) \cdot p$$
:::

. . .


**BIC**: $c(n) = \ln n$ $\quad\quad$ **AIC**: $c(n) = 2$

- $f$ is an **increasing function** of $SSR$
- $c(n) \cdot p$ is a term **penalizing** models with many variables

. . .

(Other criteria exist built on the same principle.)


## Ordering the criteria

. . .


::: {.square-def}
$$f(SSR) + c(n) \cdot p$$
:::




. . .

When $\ln n > 2$, **BIC penalizes large models more** than AIC.

. . .

Ordering criteria by the **size of the model they select** (sparsest on the left):

$$BIC \leq F\text{ test} \leq C_p \approx AIC \leq R_a^2$$

- **BIC** will favor a **smaller model** than $C_p$ or AIC
- **$R_a^2$** will tend to favor an **even larger model**

## Each criterion's best model size {.smaller}

. . .


::: {style="font-size:84%"}
One **lane per criterion** (each curve normalised separately — compare the **dot positions**, not heights). The optimum moves **right** as the penalty weakens: **BIC** picks the smallest model, **R²ₐ** the largest. Dashed line = true size $p^*=3$.
:::

```{=html}
<style>
.lm-btn{font-family:inherit;font-size:.82em;padding:.35em 1em;margin:.1em .18em;border:1px solid #bbb;border-radius:8px;background:#f4f4f4;color:#333;cursor:pointer}
.lm-row{font-size:.72em;line-height:2.3;margin:.12rem 0}
.lm-wrap{text-align:center}
</style>
<div class="lm-wrap">
  <canvas id="cuCanvas" width="620" height="300" style="background:#fff;border:1px solid #ddd;border-radius:8px;max-width:100%"></canvas>
  <div class="lm-row">noise <input id="cuNz" type="range" autocomplete="off" min="0.05" max="0.6" step="0.01" value="0.2" style="vertical-align:middle;width:140px"> <b id="cuNzv">0.20</b> &nbsp; <button class="lm-btn" id="cuNew">↻ resample</button> &nbsp;·&nbsp; optimal p: <span style="color:#7c5cff">BIC <b id="cuB">–</b></span> · <span style="color:#e67e22">AIC <b id="cuA">–</b></span> · <span style="color:#27ae60">Cₚ <b id="cuC">–</b></span> · <span style="color:#3b6fb6">R²ₐ <b id="cuR">–</b></span></div>
</div>
<script>
(function () {
  var cv = document.getElementById('cuCanvas'); if (!cv || cv.dataset.init) return; cv.dataset.init = 1;
  var c = cv.getContext('2d'), W = cv.width, H = cv.height, PMAX = 8, N = 30, Xp = [], Zp = [];
  var TRUE = [0.3, 1.0, 0.7], nzEl = document.getElementById('cuNz');
  function gauss() { var u = 0, v = 0; while (!u) u = Math.random(); while (!v) v = Math.random(); return Math.sqrt(-2 * Math.log(u)) * Math.cos(2 * Math.PI * v); }
  function vdc(n) { var r = 0, d = 1; while (n > 0) { d *= 2; r += (n % 2) / d; n = Math.floor(n / 2); } return r; }
  function regen() { Xp = []; Zp = []; for (var i = 0; i < N; i++) { Xp.push(-1 + 2 * vdc(i)); Zp.push(gauss()); } }
  function trueY(x) { var s = 0; for (var k = 0; k < TRUE.length; k++) s += TRUE[k] * Math.pow(x, k); return s; }
  function solve(M, b) { var n = b.length, a = M.map(function (r) { return r.slice(); }), bb = b.slice(), i, r, k; for (i = 0; i < n; i++) { var pv = i; for (r = i + 1; r < n; r++) if (Math.abs(a[r][i]) > Math.abs(a[pv][i])) pv = r; var t = a[i]; a[i] = a[pv]; a[pv] = t; var tb = bb[i]; bb[i] = bb[pv]; bb[pv] = tb; for (r = i + 1; r < n; r++) { var f = a[r][i] / (a[i][i] || 1e-12); for (k = i; k < n; k++) a[r][k] -= f * a[i][k]; bb[r] -= f * bb[i]; } } var x = new Array(n).fill(0); for (i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) { var s = bb[i]; for (k = i + 1; k < n; k++) s -= a[i][k] * x[k]; x[i] = s / (a[i][i] || 1e-12); } return x; }
  function ssrDeg(d, Y) { var m = d + 1, M = [], v = [], i, r, kk; for (r = 0; r < m; r++) { M.push(new Array(m).fill(0)); v.push(0); } for (i = 0; i < N; i++) { var row = []; for (kk = 0; kk < m; kk++) row.push(Math.pow(Xp[i], kk)); for (r = 0; r < m; r++) { for (kk = 0; kk < m; kk++) M[r][kk] += row[r] * row[kk]; v[r] += row[r] * Y[i]; } } for (r = 0; r < m; r++) M[r][r] += 1e-8; var b = solve(M, v), s = 0; for (i = 0; i < N; i++) { var yh = 0; for (kk = 0; kk < m; kk++) yh += b[kk] * Math.pow(Xp[i], kk); s += (Y[i] - yh) * (Y[i] - yh); } return s; }
  function argmin(a) { var b = 0; for (var i = 1; i < a.length; i++) if (a[i] < a[b]) b = i; return b; }
  function argmax(a) { var b = 0; for (var i = 1; i < a.length; i++) if (a[i] > a[b]) b = i; return b; }
  function nrm(a) { var lo = Math.min.apply(0, a), hi = Math.max.apply(0, a); return a.map(function (v) { return (v - lo) / ((hi - lo) || 1); }); }
  function draw() {
    document.getElementById('cuNzv').textContent = (+nzEl.value).toFixed(2);
    var Y = Xp.map(function (x, i) { return trueY(x) + (+nzEl.value) * Zp[i]; });
    var ssr = [], p, my = 0; Y.forEach(function (y) { my += y; }); my /= N; var SST = 0; Y.forEach(function (y) { SST += (y - my) * (y - my); });
    for (p = 1; p <= PMAX; p++) ssr.push(Math.max(ssrDeg(p - 1, Y), 1e-9));
    var sig2 = ssr[PMAX - 1] / (N - PMAX), aic = [], bic = [], cp = [], r2a = [];
    for (p = 1; p <= PMAX; p++) { var s = ssr[p - 1]; aic.push(N * Math.log(s / N) + 2 * (p + 1)); bic.push(N * Math.log(s / N) + (p + 1) * Math.log(N)); cp.push(s / sig2 - N + 2 * p); r2a.push(1 - (N - 1) / (N - p) * s / SST); }
    var lanes = [['BIC', bic, '#7c5cff', argmin(bic)], ['AIC', aic, '#e67e22', argmin(aic)], ['Cₚ', cp, '#27ae60', argmin(cp)], ['R²ₐ', r2a, '#3b6fb6', argmax(r2a)]];
    var X0 = 52, Y0 = 10, BW = W - X0 - 14, BH = H - 30, laneH = BH / 4, pad = 8;
    function PX(p) { return X0 + (p - 1) / (PMAX - 1) * BW; }
    c.clearRect(0, 0, W, H);
    c.strokeStyle = '#cfc7ef'; c.setLineDash([4, 3]); c.lineWidth = 1.5; c.beginPath(); c.moveTo(PX(3), Y0); c.lineTo(PX(3), Y0 + BH); c.stroke(); c.setLineDash([]);
    lanes.forEach(function (L, li) {
      var lt = Y0 + li * laneH, nv = nrm(L[1]);
      c.strokeStyle = '#eee'; c.lineWidth = 1; c.strokeRect(X0, lt + 2, BW, laneH - 4);
      c.fillStyle = L[2]; c.font = 'bold 12px sans-serif'; c.textAlign = 'right'; c.fillText(L[0], X0 - 6, lt + laneH / 2 + 4);
      c.strokeStyle = L[2]; c.lineWidth = 2; c.beginPath(); for (var p = 1; p <= PMAX; p++) { var x = PX(p), y = lt + laneH - pad - nv[p - 1] * (laneH - 2 * pad); p > 1 ? c.lineTo(x, y) : c.moveTo(x, y); } c.stroke();
      var op = L[3]; c.fillStyle = L[2]; c.beginPath(); c.arc(PX(op + 1), lt + laneH - pad - nv[op] * (laneH - 2 * pad), 5.5, 0, 6.2832); c.fill(); c.strokeStyle = '#fff'; c.lineWidth = 1.5; c.stroke();
    });
    c.fillStyle = '#999'; c.font = '10px sans-serif'; c.textAlign = 'center'; for (p = 1; p <= PMAX; p++) c.fillText(p, PX(p), H - 6); c.fillText('model size p', X0 + BW / 2, H - 18);
    document.getElementById('cuB').textContent = lanes[0][3] + 1; document.getElementById('cuA').textContent = lanes[1][3] + 1; document.getElementById('cuC').textContent = lanes[2][3] + 1; document.getElementById('cuR').textContent = lanes[3][3] + 1;
  }
  nzEl.addEventListener('input', draw);
  document.getElementById('cuNew').addEventListener('click', function () { regen(); draw(); });
  regen(); draw();
})();
</script>
```

## Theoretical Aspects

. . .


| Probability as $n \to \infty$ &nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; | BIC | $C_p$, AIC, $R_a^2$ |
|---|-----|------|
| $\mathbb{P}$(selects model **smaller** than true) | $\to 0$ | $\to 0$ |
| $\mathbb{P}$(selects model **larger** than true) | $\to 0$ | $\not\to 0$ |
| $\mathbb{P}$(selects **correct model**) | $\to 1$ | $\not\to 1$ |

. . .

**BIC is asymptotically consistent**, while other criteria tend to overfit.


## AIC vs BIC: consistency as n grows {.smaller}

. . .


::: {style="font-size:84%"}
Repeat the experiment (best-subset over $1,x,\dots,x^4$, truth $=\{1,x,x^2\}$) and record how often each criterion **misses a term**, **recovers exactly**, or **over-selects**. As $n$ grows, **BIC → exact**, while **AIC keeps over-selecting**.
:::

```{=html}
<style>
.lm-btn{font-family:inherit;font-size:.82em;padding:.35em 1em;margin:.1em .18em;border:1px solid #bbb;border-radius:8px;background:#f4f4f4;color:#333;cursor:pointer}
.lm-row{font-size:.72em;line-height:2.3;margin:.12rem 0}
.lm-wrap{text-align:center}
.lm-sw{display:inline-block;width:11px;height:11px;border-radius:2px;vertical-align:middle}
</style>
<div class="lm-wrap">
  <canvas id="csCanvas" width="560" height="290" style="background:#fff;border:1px solid #ddd;border-radius:8px;max-width:100%"></canvas>
  <div class="lm-row">sample size n <input id="csN" type="range" autocomplete="off" min="20" max="150" step="2" value="30" style="vertical-align:middle;width:170px"> <b id="csNv">30</b> &nbsp;·&nbsp; noise <input id="csS" type="range" autocomplete="off" min="0.1" max="0.6" step="0.02" value="0.3" style="vertical-align:middle;width:90px"> <b id="csSv">0.30</b></div>
  <div class="lm-row"><span class="lm-sw" style="background:#e74c3c"></span> missed &nbsp; <span class="lm-sw" style="background:#27ae60"></span> exact &nbsp; <span class="lm-sw" style="background:#e67e22"></span> over-selected &nbsp;·&nbsp; <span style="color:#888">300 repetitions</span></div>
</div>
<script>
(function () {
  var cv = document.getElementById('csCanvas'); if (!cv || cv.dataset.init) return; cv.dataset.init = 1;
  var c = cv.getContext('2d'), W = cv.width, H = cv.height, PMAX = 5, M = 300, TRUE = [0.3, 1.0, 0.7], TRUESET = [0, 1, 2];
  var nEl = document.getElementById('csN'), sEl = document.getElementById('csS');
  function gauss() { var u = 0, v = 0; while (!u) u = Math.random(); while (!v) v = Math.random(); return Math.sqrt(-2 * Math.log(u)) * Math.cos(2 * Math.PI * v); }
  function vdc(k) { var r = 0, d = 1; while (k > 0) { d *= 2; r += (k % 2) / d; k = Math.floor(k / 2); } return r; }
  function trueY(x) { var s = 0; for (var k = 0; k < TRUE.length; k++) s += TRUE[k] * Math.pow(x, k); return s; }
  function solve(Mx, b) { var n = b.length, a = Mx.map(function (r) { return r.slice(); }), bb = b.slice(), i, r, k; for (i = 0; i < n; i++) { var pv = i; for (r = i + 1; r < n; r++) if (Math.abs(a[r][i]) > Math.abs(a[pv][i])) pv = r; var t = a[i]; a[i] = a[pv]; a[pv] = t; var tb = bb[i]; bb[i] = bb[pv]; bb[pv] = tb; for (r = i + 1; r < n; r++) { var f = a[r][i] / (a[i][i] || 1e-12); for (k = i; k < n; k++) a[r][k] -= f * a[i][k]; bb[r] -= f * bb[i]; } } var x = new Array(n).fill(0); for (i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) { var s = bb[i]; for (k = i + 1; k < n; k++) s -= a[i][k] * x[k]; x[i] = s / (a[i][i] || 1e-12); } return x; }
  function ssr(pows, X, Y, n) { var m = pows.length, M2 = [], v = [], i, r, k; for (r = 0; r < m; r++) { M2.push(new Array(m).fill(0)); v.push(0); } for (i = 0; i < n; i++) { var row = pows.map(function (pw) { return Math.pow(X[i], pw); }); for (r = 0; r < m; r++) { for (k = 0; k < m; k++) M2[r][k] += row[r] * row[k]; v[r] += row[r] * Y[i]; } } for (r = 0; r < m; r++) M2[r][r] += 1e-8; var b = solve(M2, v), s = 0; for (i = 0; i < n; i++) { var yh = 0; for (k = 0; k < m; k++) yh += b[k] * Math.pow(X[i], pows[k]); s += (Y[i] - yh) * (Y[i] - yh); } return s; }
  var SUBS = []; for (var mk = 0; mk < (1 << (PMAX - 1)); mk++) { var s = [0]; for (var i = 0; i < PMAX - 1; i++) if (mk & (1 << i)) s.push(i + 1); SUBS.push(s); }
  function classify(sel) { var set = {}; sel.forEach(function (k) { set[k] = 1; }); for (var i = 0; i < TRUESET.length; i++) if (!set[TRUESET[i]]) return 0; return sel.length > TRUESET.length ? 2 : 1; }   // 0 missed, 1 exact, 2 over
  function run(n, sig) {
    var X = []; for (var i = 0; i < n; i++) X.push(-1 + 2 * vdc(i));
    var aic = [0, 0, 0], bic = [0, 0, 0], rep;
    for (rep = 0; rep < M; rep++) {
      var Y = X.map(function (x) { return trueY(x) + sig * gauss(); });
      var aB = null, aV = Infinity, bB = null, bV = Infinity;
      for (var j = 0; j < SUBS.length; j++) { var p = SUBS[j].length, s = Math.max(ssr(SUBS[j], X, Y, n), 1e-12), ln = n * Math.log(s / n); var av = ln + 2 * (p + 1), bv = ln + (p + 1) * Math.log(n); if (av < aV) { aV = av; aB = SUBS[j]; } if (bv < bV) { bV = bv; bB = SUBS[j]; } }
      aic[classify(aB)]++; bic[classify(bB)]++;
    }
    return { aic: aic.map(function (x) { return x / M; }), bic: bic.map(function (x) { return x / M; }) };
  }
  function draw() {
    var n = +nEl.value, sig = +sEl.value; document.getElementById('csNv').textContent = n; document.getElementById('csSv').textContent = sig.toFixed(2);
    var R = run(n, sig), cols = ['#e74c3c', '#27ae60', '#e67e22'], top = 16, bh = H - 54, bw = 90;
    c.clearRect(0, 0, W, H);
    [['AIC', R.aic, W * 0.32], ['BIC', R.bic, W * 0.68]].forEach(function (g) {
      var name = g[0], pr = g[1], cx = g[2], y = top;
      for (var s = 0; s < 3; s++) { var seg = pr[s] * bh; c.fillStyle = cols[s]; c.fillRect(cx - bw / 2, y, bw, seg); if (pr[s] > 0.06) { c.fillStyle = '#fff'; c.font = 'bold 12px sans-serif'; c.textAlign = 'center'; c.fillText(Math.round(pr[s] * 100) + '%', cx, y + seg / 2 + 4); } y += seg; }
      c.fillStyle = '#222'; c.font = 'bold 14px sans-serif'; c.textAlign = 'center'; c.fillText(name, cx, top + bh + 20);
    });
    c.strokeStyle = '#eee'; c.strokeRect(8, top, W - 16, bh);
  }
  var pend = false; function sched() { if (pend) return; pend = true; requestAnimationFrame(function () { pend = false; draw(); }); }
  nEl.addEventListener('input', sched); sEl.addEventListener('input', sched);
  draw();
})();
</script>
```

# Automatic Selection

## Exhaustive search

. . .

Given $p_{\max}$ available explanatory variables:

- **Tempting approach**: Test all possible sub-models
- **Selection**: Keep the one with lowest BIC (or other criterion)
- **Computational cost**: $2^{p_{\max}}$ models to test (that's a lot!)

. . .

If $p_{\max}$ is not too large, this remains feasible.

**R function**: `regsubsets` from `leaps` library

## Exhaustive search: a warning

. . .

::: {.callout-warning}
## Important Warning
Automatic selection does **not guarantee** that the selected model is good.

It's simply the **best model** [according to the chosen criterion]{style="background-color: orange;"}.

The selected model may be **bad** in terms of:

- Explanatory power
- Multicollinearity problems  
- Heteroscedasticity issues
- Auto-correlation problems
:::


## Stepwise: backward

. . .

If $p_{\max}$ is too large for exhaustive search:

. . .

[**Stepwise Backward**]{style="background-color: yellow;"} (according to chosen criterion, e.g., BIC):

- Start with largest model ($p_{\max}$ variables)
- Remove least significant variable
- Repeat: remove remaining least significant variable
- Stop when no removal improves the model

## Stepwise: forward & hybrid

. . .


If $p_{\max}$ is too large for exhaustive search:

. . .

[**Stepwise Forward**]{style="background-color: yellow;"}:

- Start with smallest model (constant only)
- Add most significant variable at each step

. . .

[**Stepwise Backward (or Forward) Hybrid**]{style="background-color: yellow;"}:

- Like backward (or forward), but also try **adding** (or **removing**) a variable at each step


## Automatic selection in action {.smaller}

. . .


::: {style="font-size:84%"}
Pick an **algorithm** and a **criterion**. For *forward*/*backward*, step through the greedy path with **◀ / ▶**; the criterion's best step (★) is the selected model. Truth $=\{1,x,x^2\}$ (★ under columns). BIC stays parsimonious; $R^2_a$ tends to over-select.
:::

```{=html}
<style>
.lm-btn{font-family:inherit;font-size:.8em;padding:.32em .9em;margin:.1em .15em;border:1px solid #bbb;border-radius:8px;background:#f4f4f4;color:#333;cursor:pointer}
.lm-btn.on{background:#3b6fb6;color:#fff;border-color:#3b6fb6}
.lm-btn:disabled{opacity:.4;cursor:default}
.lm-row{font-size:.68em;line-height:2.1;margin:.08rem 0}
.lm-wrap{text-align:center}
</style>
<div class="lm-wrap" id="saWrap">
  <canvas id="saCanvas" width="660" height="280" style="background:#fff;border:1px solid #ddd;border-radius:8px;max-width:100%"></canvas>
  <div class="lm-row">
    algo: <button class="lm-btn" data-algo="best">best subset</button><button class="lm-btn on" data-algo="fwd">forward</button><button class="lm-btn" data-algo="bwd">backward</button>
    &nbsp; criterion: <button class="lm-btn on" data-crit="bic">BIC</button><button class="lm-btn" data-crit="aic">AIC</button><button class="lm-btn" data-crit="cp">Cₚ</button><button class="lm-btn" data-crit="r2a">R²ₐ</button>
  </div>
  <div class="lm-row">
    <button class="lm-btn" id="saPrev">◀ prev</button><button class="lm-btn" id="saNext">next ▶</button><button class="lm-btn" id="saOpt">optimal ★</button>
    &nbsp; noise <input id="saNz" type="range" autocomplete="off" min="0" max="0.6" step="0.01" value="0.2" style="vertical-align:middle;width:90px"> <b id="saNzv">0.20</b> &nbsp; <button class="lm-btn" id="saNew">↻ resample</button>
  </div>
  <div class="lm-row">
    <b id="saStep">–</b> &nbsp;·&nbsp; selected: <b id="saSel">–</b> &nbsp;·&nbsp; <b id="saCritv">–</b> &nbsp;·&nbsp; <span id="saRec">–</span>
  </div>
</div>
<script>
(function () {
  var cv = document.getElementById('saCanvas'); if (!cv || cv.dataset.init) return; cv.dataset.init = 1;
  var c = cv.getContext('2d'), W = cv.width, H = cv.height, PMAX = 8, N = 30, Xp = [], Zp = [];
  var LBL = ['1', 'x', 'x²', 'x³', 'x⁴', 'x⁵', 'x⁶', 'x⁷'], TRUE = [0.3, 1.0, 0.7], TRUESET = [0, 1, 2];
  var algo = 'fwd', crit = 'bic', path = [], selIdx = 0, step = 0, Yc, SST, sig2, nzEl = document.getElementById('saNz');
  function gauss() { var u = 0, v = 0; while (!u) u = Math.random(); while (!v) v = Math.random(); return Math.sqrt(-2 * Math.log(u)) * Math.cos(2 * Math.PI * v); }
  function vdc(n) { var r = 0, d = 1; while (n > 0) { d *= 2; r += (n % 2) / d; n = Math.floor(n / 2); } return r; }
  function regen() { Xp = []; Zp = []; for (var i = 0; i < N; i++) { Xp.push(-1 + 2 * vdc(i)); Zp.push(gauss()); } }
  function trueY(x) { var s = 0; for (var k = 0; k < TRUE.length; k++) s += TRUE[k] * Math.pow(x, k); return s; }
  function solve(M, b) { var n = b.length, a = M.map(function (r) { return r.slice(); }), bb = b.slice(), i, r, k; for (i = 0; i < n; i++) { var pv = i; for (r = i + 1; r < n; r++) if (Math.abs(a[r][i]) > Math.abs(a[pv][i])) pv = r; var t = a[i]; a[i] = a[pv]; a[pv] = t; var tb = bb[i]; bb[i] = bb[pv]; bb[pv] = tb; for (r = i + 1; r < n; r++) { var f = a[r][i] / (a[i][i] || 1e-12); for (k = i; k < n; k++) a[r][k] -= f * a[i][k]; bb[r] -= f * bb[i]; } } var x = new Array(n).fill(0); for (i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) { var s = bb[i]; for (k = i + 1; k < n; k++) s -= a[i][k] * x[k]; x[i] = s / (a[i][i] || 1e-12); } return x; }
  function fit(pows) { var m = pows.length, M = [], v = [], i, r, k; for (r = 0; r < m; r++) { M.push(new Array(m).fill(0)); v.push(0); } for (i = 0; i < N; i++) { var row = pows.map(function (pw) { return Math.pow(Xp[i], pw); }); for (r = 0; r < m; r++) { for (k = 0; k < m; k++) M[r][k] += row[r] * row[k]; v[r] += row[r] * Yc[i]; } } for (r = 0; r < m; r++) M[r][r] += 1e-7; var beta = solve(M, v), ssr = 0; for (i = 0; i < N; i++) { var yh = 0; for (k = 0; k < m; k++) yh += beta[k] * Math.pow(Xp[i], pows[k]); ssr += (Yc[i] - yh) * (Yc[i] - yh); } return { beta: beta, ssr: ssr }; }
  function prep() { Yc = Xp.map(function (x, i) { return trueY(x) + (+nzEl.value) * Zp[i]; }); var my = 0; Yc.forEach(function (y) { my += y; }); my /= N; SST = 0; Yc.forEach(function (y) { SST += (y - my) * (y - my); }); sig2 = fit([0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7]).ssr / (N - PMAX); }
  function critVal(pows) { var ssr = Math.max(fit(pows).ssr, 1e-12), p = pows.length; if (crit === 'aic') return N * Math.log(ssr / N) + 2 * (p + 1); if (crit === 'bic') return N * Math.log(ssr / N) + (p + 1) * Math.log(N); if (crit === 'cp') return ssr / sig2 - N + 2 * p; return -(1 - (N - 1) / (N - p) * ssr / SST); }
  function build() {
    prep();
    if (algo === 'best') {
      var opt = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7], best = [0], bv = Infinity, i;
      for (var mk = 0; mk < (1 << 7); mk++) { var s = [0]; for (i = 0; i < 7; i++) if (mk & (1 << i)) s.push(opt[i]); var v = critVal(s); if (v < bv) { bv = v; best = s; } }
      path = [best.sort(function (a, b) { return a - b; })]; selIdx = 0;
    } else if (algo === 'fwd') {
      var cur = [0], rem = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7]; path = [cur.slice()];
      while (rem.length) { var bi = 0, bw = Infinity; for (i = 0; i < rem.length; i++) { var vv = critVal(cur.concat([rem[i]])); if (vv < bw) { bw = vv; bi = i; } } cur.push(rem[bi]); rem.splice(bi, 1); path.push(cur.slice().sort(function (a, b) { return a - b; })); }
    } else {
      var cb = [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7]; path = [cb.slice()];
      while (cb.length > 1) { var ri = 1, rw = Infinity; for (i = 1; i < cb.length; i++) { var t = cb.slice(); t.splice(i, 1); var v2 = critVal(t); if (v2 < rw) { rw = v2; ri = i; } } cb.splice(ri, 1); path.push(cb.slice().sort(function (a, b) { return a - b; })); }
    }
    if (algo !== 'best') { selIdx = 0; var bestv = Infinity; for (var j = 0; j < path.length; j++) { var cvj = critVal(path[j]); if (cvj < bestv) { bestv = cvj; selIdx = j; } } }
    step = selIdx;
  }
  function draw() {
    document.getElementById('saNzv').textContent = (+nzEl.value).toFixed(2);
    var sel = path[step], selSet = {}; sel.forEach(function (k) { selSet[k] = 1; });
    var f = fit(sel), beta = f.beta, k;
    c.clearRect(0, 0, W, H);
    var mx0 = 16, mtop = 16, mh = 190, cw = (236 - mx0) / PMAX;
    c.font = '11px sans-serif'; c.textAlign = 'center'; c.fillStyle = '#777'; c.fillText('columns of X (powers)', mx0 + PMAX * cw / 2 - 4, mtop - 5);
    for (k = 0; k < PMAX; k++) { c.fillStyle = selSet[k] ? '#3b6fb6' : '#c0508f'; c.globalAlpha = selSet[k] ? 0.9 : 0.45; c.fillRect(mx0 + k * cw + 1, mtop, cw - 3, mh); c.globalAlpha = 1; c.fillStyle = '#333'; c.fillText(LBL[k], mx0 + k * cw + cw / 2, mtop + mh + 14); if (TRUESET.indexOf(k) >= 0) { c.fillStyle = '#e8a200'; c.fillText('★', mx0 + k * cw + cw / 2, mtop + mh + 27); } }
    var rx0 = 300, rx1 = W - 12, XL = -1.12, XH = 1.12, YL = -1.9, YH = 1.9, i;
    function PX(x) { return rx0 + (x - XL) / (XH - XL) * (rx1 - rx0); } function PY(y) { return H - 22 - (y - YL) / (YH - YL) * (H - 34); }
    c.strokeStyle = '#ddd'; c.strokeRect(rx0, 12, rx1 - rx0, H - 34); c.fillStyle = '#777'; c.textAlign = 'center'; c.fillText('current-model fit', (rx0 + rx1) / 2, H - 6);
    c.strokeStyle = '#bbb'; c.lineWidth = 1.5; c.setLineDash([5, 4]); c.beginPath(); for (i = 0; i <= 200; i++) { var x = XL + (XH - XL) * i / 200; i ? c.lineTo(PX(x), PY(trueY(x))) : c.moveTo(PX(x), PY(trueY(x))); } c.stroke(); c.setLineDash([]);
    c.save(); c.beginPath(); c.rect(rx0, 12, rx1 - rx0, H - 34); c.clip();
    c.strokeStyle = '#e74c3c'; c.lineWidth = 2.5; c.beginPath(); for (i = 0; i <= 220; i++) { var xx = XL + (XH - XL) * i / 220, yh = 0; for (var j = 0; j < sel.length; j++) yh += beta[j] * Math.pow(xx, sel[j]); i ? c.lineTo(PX(xx), PY(yh)) : c.moveTo(PX(xx), PY(yh)); } c.stroke();
    c.fillStyle = 'rgba(44,62,80,0.7)'; for (i = 0; i < N; i++) { c.beginPath(); c.arc(PX(Xp[i]), PY(Yc[i]), 2.8, 0, 6.2832); c.fill(); }
    c.restore();
    // readouts
    var isBest = algo === 'best';
    document.getElementById('saPrev').disabled = isBest || step <= 0; document.getElementById('saNext').disabled = isBest || step >= path.length - 1; document.getElementById('saOpt').disabled = isBest || step === selIdx;
    var stepTxt = isBest ? 'best subset' : ('step ' + (step + 1) + '/' + path.length + (step === selIdx ? ' ★ selected' : ''));
    if (!isBest && step > 0) { var prev = path[step - 1], moved = (step > selIdx || sel.length > prev.length) ? sel.filter(function (x) { return prev.indexOf(x) < 0; }) : prev.filter(function (x) { return sel.indexOf(x) < 0; }); var sign = sel.length > prev.length ? '+' : '−'; if (moved.length) stepTxt += ' (' + sign + LBL[moved[0]] + ')'; }
    document.getElementById('saStep').textContent = stepTxt;
    document.getElementById('saSel').textContent = '{' + sel.map(function (k) { return LBL[k]; }).join(', ') + '}';
    var cl = { aic: 'AIC', bic: 'BIC', cp: 'Cₚ', r2a: 'R²ₐ' }[crit], cvv = critVal(sel); document.getElementById('saCritv').textContent = cl + '=' + (crit === 'r2a' ? (-cvv).toFixed(3) : cvv.toFixed(1));
    var exact = sel.length === TRUESET.length && TRUESET.every(function (k) { return selSet[k]; });
    document.getElementById('saRec').innerHTML = exact ? '<span style="color:#1b7a3a">= true model ✓</span>' : (sel.length > TRUESET.length ? '<span style="color:#e67e22">over-selected</span>' : '<span style="color:#e74c3c">missing a term</span>');
  }
  function recompute() { build(); draw(); }
  Array.prototype.forEach.call(document.querySelectorAll('#saWrap .lm-btn[data-algo]'), function (b) { b.addEventListener('click', function () { algo = b.dataset.algo; Array.prototype.forEach.call(document.querySelectorAll('#saWrap .lm-btn[data-algo]'), function (x) { x.classList.toggle('on', x === b); }); recompute(); }); });
  Array.prototype.forEach.call(document.querySelectorAll('#saWrap .lm-btn[data-crit]'), function (b) { b.addEventListener('click', function () { crit = b.dataset.crit; Array.prototype.forEach.call(document.querySelectorAll('#saWrap .lm-btn[data-crit]'), function (x) { x.classList.toggle('on', x === b); }); recompute(); }); });
  document.getElementById('saPrev').addEventListener('click', function () { if (step > 0) { step--; draw(); } });
  document.getElementById('saNext').addEventListener('click', function () { if (step < path.length - 1) { step++; draw(); } });
  document.getElementById('saOpt').addEventListener('click', function () { step = selIdx; draw(); });
  nzEl.addEventListener('input', recompute);
  document.getElementById('saNew').addEventListener('click', function () { regen(); recompute(); });
  regen(); recompute();
})();
</script>
```

## Limitations and Characteristics

. . .

**Stepwise procedures** do **not explore all possible sub-models**:

- May **miss** the best model

. . .

**Speed comparison**:

- **Forward**: fastest (small models are quicker to estimate)
- **Hybrid procedures**: slower, but explore more possible models

## R Implementation

. . .

**Function**: `step` with option `direction`:

- `"backward"` or `"forward"` or `"both"`
- **Default criterion**: AIC (`k = 2`)
- **For BIC**: use `k = ln(n)`

. . .

The option `k` corresponds to the penalty $c(n)$ introduced earlier.

## Next

. . .


[ANOVA / ANCOVA](anova_ancova.qmd)
